source.title: Dissecting the evolvability landscape of the CalB active site toward aromatic substrates

initial_request.research_problem: Engineering CalB lipase to improve activity toward bulky aromatic substrates (methyl cinnamate and methyl salicylate) due to poor native recognition, while overcoming limitations of high-throughput screening for transesterification.
initial_request.known_context: CalB has poor activity on bulky aromatic substrates; high-throughput screening for transesterification is difficult.
constraints: High-throughput screening for transesterification is challenging
quantifiable_goals: Increase transesterification activity toward methyl cinnamate and methyl salicylate

evolution_trajectory.state_before: CalB has poor activity on bulky aromatic substrates and high-throughput screening for transesterification is difficult.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to efficiently engineer CalB for improved activity on bulky aromatics given screening limitations.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A combinatorial semi-rational approach (ISRD) combining virtual docking, iterative saturation mutagenesis, and RIN analysis can design smaller, smarter mutant libraries.
evolution_trajectory.observation: ISRD approach proposed to partly overcome screening challenge.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to identify mutable residues using RIN analysis.

evidence.quote_or_span: the functional analysis and identification of active variants among such high numbers of mutational possibilities is not a trivial task
we describe a combinatorial semi-rational approach to partly overcome this challenge and help design smaller and smarter mutant libraries

By adapting a liquid medium transesterification assay in organic solvent conditions with a combination of virtual docking, iterative saturation mutagenesis, and residue interaction network (RIN) analysis

evolution_trajectory.state_before: ISRD hypothesis formulated; need to select mutable residues.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which residues are likely to yield beneficial mutations?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Residues with low betweenness centrality in RIN are more likely to accommodate beneficial mutations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use RIN analysis of previous CalB mutations to guide selection.
evolution_trajectory.observation: More than 80% of previous beneficial mutations had low betweenness centrality.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Select WT CalB as starting template based on similar performance to L278P variant.

evidence.quote_or_span: We found that more than 80% of previous beneficial mutations that either increase CalB activity or improve protein stability exhibit low values of betweenness centrality measure
Due to similar synthetic performance relative to the previously reported variant CalB-L278P, we selected WT CalB as a proper starting template for all future protein engineering steps

evolution_trajectory.state_before: WT CalB selected as starting template.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need efficient expression system for CalB.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: E. coli Rosetta (DE3) with SB medium, 0.1 mM IPTG, 16°C will yield high CalB expression.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Most efficient bacterial CalB producer identified.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Develop screening assay for transesterification activity.

evidence.quote_or_span: the most efficient bacterial CalB producer was found to be E. coli Rosetta (DE3) grown on SB medium (0.1 mM IPTG induction, 16°C)

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a high-throughput screening assay for transesterification.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A liquid medium assay using vinyl substrates and MBTH detection can screen transesterification activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop and standardize liquid medium transesterification assay.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Assay adapted and standardized for CalB synthetic activity screening.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Select active-site mutational hot spots using docking and RIN.

evidence.quote_or_span: we adapted and standardized a liquid medium CalB synthetic activity screening assay using vinyl analogs to mimic cinnamic and salicylic acid compound recognition
Substrates were used on a 3:1 ratio of methanol (Sigma) and vinyl cinnamate (or vinyl salicylate) (Aldrich), two analogs of the target substrates cinnamic acid and salicylic acid.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Screening assay ready; need to select positions for mutagenesis.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Positions with high docking energy contributions and low betweenness centrality are promising targets.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Combine virtual docking energy ranking and RIN betweenness centrality to select hot spots.

evidence.quote_or_span: Identification and selection of active-site mutational 'hot spots' by convergence of two approaches. First, identification of residue positions that stabilize the ligand-enzyme complex is achieved by virtual docking, after which involvement of those positions in the maintenance of structure and topology of the active-site cavity is performed by a Residue Interaction Network (RIN) analysis

amino acid replacements exhibiting high energy contributions to binary complex stabilization and low values of betweenness centrality measure were selected as promising positional targets for mutagenesis

For the first generation of mutants (G1), we designed 6 distinct site-saturation mutagenesis libraries at CalB positions 40, 134, 138, 154, 157, and 189, which were then individually screened against both substrate analogs. These semi-randomized positions were selected according to favorable energy contributions with respect to enzyme-substrate complex stabilization

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to create mutant libraries for screening.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Site-saturation mutagenesis at selected positions will yield variants with improved activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design site-saturation mutagenesis libraries at positions 40, 134, 138, 154, 157, 189.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Screen G1 libraries for improved methyl cinnamate transesterification.

evidence.quote_or_span: For the first generation of mutants (G1), we designed 6 distinct site-saturation mutagenesis libraries at CalB positions 40, 134, 138, 154, 157, and 189, which were then individually screened against both substrate analogs. These semi-randomized positions were selected according to favorable energy contributions with respect to enzyme-substrate complex stabilization
Based on virtual docking predictions, an individual saturation mutagenesis library was designed for each selected position in the active site of CalB.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which G1 variants improve methyl cinnamate transesterification?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Variants at positions T138 and D134 will show increased activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen G1 libraries against methyl cinnamate.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 6 variants with increased activity; T138G, T138A, T138S, T138N show up to 4-fold improvement.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Expand mutational exploration to additional positions for G2.

evidence.quote_or_span: the most active methyl cinnamate G1 variants were observed for randomized positions T138 and D134 (Fig. 3A), where 6 variants showed increased transesterification activity relative to WT. The most efficient G1 variants are T138G, T138A, T138S, and T138N, displaying up to 4-fold increase in methyl cinnamate production relative to WT CalB

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Will combining G1 and G2 mutations yield synergistic improvements?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Adding G2 mutations V190A or D134S to T138G will further increase activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen double mutants T138G-V190A and T138G-D134S.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Double mutants show nearly 6-fold improvement over WT.

evidence.quote_or_span: The addition of G2-selected mutations (V190A and D134S) to the G1-T138G template allowed selection of double mutants displaying production yields nearly 6-fold that of the WT conversion rate for the same compound

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Methyl cinnamate trajectory successful; methyl salicylate activity still poor.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can CalB activity on methyl salicylate be improved?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutations V154L and L278A may improve methyl salicylate transesterification.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Double mutant V154L-L278A shows 3.6-fold increase; WT had <0.5% conversion.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design G3 libraries combining positive mutations.

evidence.quote_or_span: we nevertheless selected a double mutant (V154L-L278A) exhibiting 3.6-fold increase in catalytic activity relative to WT CalB. Noteworthy is the fact that WT CalB shows negligible synthetic activity against vinyl salicylate (<0.5% conversion)

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Will combining D134S and V190A with T138G be beneficial?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Triple mutant T138G-V190A-D134S may show additive effects.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen G3 libraries for methyl cinnamate activity.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Combining D134S and V190A with T138G had deleterious effects.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze why combination was deleterious using RIN and NMA.

evidence.quote_or_span: active G2 substitutions (D134S and V190A) in the context of the favorable G1 single mutation T138G had deleterious effects on the performance of CalB towards methyl cinnamate synthesis

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Deleterious effects explained; need to understand cavity organization.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How is the catalytic cavity organized to guide further engineering?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The catalytic cavity is divided into three main residue modules.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform RIN analysis to identify residue modules.
evolution_trajectory.observation: RIN analysis revealed the catalytic cavity is divided into three main residue modules.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Examine structural basis of improved double mutants.

evidence.quote_or_span: Our RIN analysis shows that the catalytic cavity of CalB is divided into three main residue modules, which describe clustering interactions defined by the Linkland algorithm

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Modular organization identified; need to understand how double mutants improve activity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How do T138G-V190A and V154L-L278A enhance substrate binding?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Both double mutants stabilize substrate aromatic rings, aligning the carbonyl moiety near S105.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Both double mutants T138G-V190A and V154L-L278A stabilize substrate aromatic rings, allowing proximal alignment of the carbonyl moiety 2–4 Å from catalytic S105.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate overall approach and summarize findings.

evidence.quote_or_span: Both double mutants T138G-V190A and V154L-L278A favor stabilization of substrate aromatic rings, allowing proximal alignment of the carbonyl moiety 2–4 Å away from the catalytic S105 (dashed line).

validation_methods: Screening of 60-66 clones per library over 3 generations
Comparison to WT and previously reported variants
success_verification.final_conclusion: ISRD approach yielded 12 improved mutants from screening only 0.308% of sequence space, with best variants showing up to 6-fold improvement for methyl cinnamate and 3.6-fold for methyl salicylate.
limitations: Only 11 out of 23 active-site cavity positions explored
G3 variants did not outperform best G2 candidates
Deleterious effects observed when combining certain beneficial mutations
